verdict_reasoning: EREG encodes epiregulin, a secreted peptide growth factor of the EGF family. It is synthesized as a transmembrane precursor (pro-epiregulin) that undergoes ectodomain shedding by ADAM proteases, releasing the mature soluble ligand. The shed soluble form circulates and binds EGFR/ERBB4 on target cells, but this is transient non-covalent receptor recruitment — the protein body itself has no stable surface anchor after cleavage. The pro-form is transiently membrane-anchored during processing, but the biologically active and dominant form is the shed ectodomain. No stable surface-anchored isoform is documented. Verdict: secreted_only.
